task_completion.description: Navigate to the workout routine creation form in wger.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the wger fitness manager interface with a form, page, or modal open to add a new workout routine.
The text '5x5 Strength Program' is visible inside the 'Name' or 'Title' input field of the workout creation form.
The text 'Classic powerlifting progression for building raw strength' is visible inside the description text area of the workout creation form.

task_completion.description: Submit the form to save the new workout routine.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cursor clicking a 'Save', 'Submit', or 'Add' button associated with the filled-out workout form.

task_completion.description: Verify the new workout routine was successfully created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message, the workout details page, or the workout dashboard showing '5x5 Strength Program' in the list of saved workouts.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the wger application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show a standard web browser navigating to localhost, rather than terminal commands (like curl) or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual wger UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs for name and description are entered into the actual wger web forms, not a separate text editor or notepad.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the success state.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open to manually edit the DOM to show the workout name.
